design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character → traverse level with platforming and combat → defeat boss or reach exit → switch character for next stage to leverage different abilities
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Character selection creates dynamic difficulty—each stage layout caters to specific character strengths, forcing players to adapt strategy and learn multiple playstyles to progress
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a roster of Marvel X-Men, each with distinct superhero powers and traversal mechanics, adapting tactics stage-by-stage to overcome obstacles uniquely suited to different characters
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game enforces character versatility through stage design that penalizes single-character reliance. No 'easy mode' character exists—every stage has shortcut pickups and layouts requiring specific traversal abilities (wall-climb, flight, teleport), making character switching not optional but mandatory for optimal progression and resource collection

difficulty.lives_system: Players begin with nine lives total. No mechanism to earn additional lives or continues exists. Loss of all lives triggers game over with complete progress reset to start
difficulty.continues_system: No continues system; game over results in full restart from beginning
difficulty.checkpointing: No mid-stage checkpoints. Each stage must be completed in one attempt or player loses a life and restarts the stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through character selection. Player can swap characters between stage attempts to match character strengths to stage design. Health management (10 HP max) creates resource tension—collecting health pickups becomes strategically crucial. Mutant attack power scales with health (stronger at 9+ HP), incentivizing aggressive play and risk management

progression.upgrade_system: No persistent upgrades. Character power scaling tied to current health status only (charged attacks, powered-up mutant powers at full health)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Magneto unlocked after completing third level, adding a sixth playable character with unique mechanics (no melee attacks, unlimited energy blasts, hovering, electromagnetic orb ranged attack)
replayability_hooks: Character roster variety incentivizes replaying stages with different characters to discover optimal routes and shortcuts
Stage layout variations based on character selection provide different challenge configurations
Nine-life constraint creates time-attack-like tension encouraging skill improvement
Health pickup placement varies by stage, encouraging exploration with traversal-capable characters

world_building_techniques: Cold open—game begins immediately with first stage; title sequence and opening narration appear only after completing stage one, subverting player expectations
Voiced narration through Professor X and Cerebro communication framework establishes stakes (Phalanx threat, virus contamination)

Stage progression follows comic book story arc: Sentinel facility → Avalon → Apocalypse's base → Savage Land → Phalanx ship → clone factory → final confrontation with Phalanx clones of X-Men themselves

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters function as stage gates; minor bosses appear at stage ends, major bosses comprise entire stages. Boss design emphasizes pattern recognition with character-specific vulnerabilities—certain characters have traversal or attack advantages against specific bosses
notable_bosses: Phalanx clones of playable X-Men (final confrontation)

danger_signals: Technorganic alien assimilation threat (Phalanx virus spreading globally)
Phalanx clones of X-Men themselves as final boss (identity threat)
environmental_storytelling: Sentinel manufacturing facility corruption (alien technology takeover)
Avalon setting implies refuge turned battleground
Savage Land contrast (wilderness becomes threat zone)
